content: labelme establishes the standard batch-annotation UX baseline: Open Dir loads a folder, a File List sidebar lets you jump between images and see which are done, Ctrl+S saves per-image annotation to a JSON sidecar, Ctrl+Z undoes the last polygon point, and shortcuts are user-customizable via ~/.labelmerc. Because each image's annotation is saved as its own file immediately, resuming is implicit — reopening the folder shows already-labeled images distinctly in the file list. This per-image-sidecar-file pattern (rather than one big session file) is the simplest way to get free resume/progress-tracking.
